creator: jumper99@gmx.de
text:
> email bounced:
>
> jumper99@gmx.de
> SMTP error from remote mail server after RCPT TO:<jumper99@gmx.de>:
> host mx0.gmx.net [213.165.64.100]: 550-5.7.1 {mx028} The recipient
> does not accept mails from 'FreeBSD.org' over foreign mailservers.
> 550-5.7.1 According to the domain's SPF record your host
> 'XXX.247.195.XXX' is not a designated sender.
> 550 5.7.1 ( http://portal.gmx.net/serverrules )

Well it's FreeBSD.org that configured SPF. And according to that DNS your IP 
is not allowed to send email:

[root@BSDHelmut ~]# host -t TXT freebsd.org
freebsd.org descriptive text "v=spf1 ip4:69.147.83.53 ip4:69.147.83.54 
ip6:2001:4f8:fff6::35 ip6:2001:4f8:fff6::36 ~all"
[root@BSDHelmut ~]#

> Show a complete error message, portupgrade version, portupgrade command
> line (what options did you use) and an affected package.
> And I can't find 'see also' link.

Please see the discussion here:
http://groups.google.de/group/muc.lists.freebsd.ports/msg/4f6d4f1e9505c83f
The problem only exists when you do a portupgrade -PP. Updating the port by 
recompiling works fine (portupgrade without '-PP').

If you still need more information please contact me again.

Helmut
